source_clause_title: 4.10. Set-off.  In addition to any rights of set-off a Party may have as a matter of law or otherwise

entities.name: Non-Defaulting Party
entities.legal_role: The party not in breach of the agreement who gains the right to set off obligations upon an Event of Default.
entities.evidence_quote: the Non-Defaulting Party shall have the right (but shall not be obligated to) without prior notice to the Defaulting Party or any other person to set-off any obligation of the Defaulting Party owed to the Non-Defaulting Party under this Agreement

entities.name: Defaulting Party
entities.legal_role: The party in breach of the agreement whose obligations may be set off by the Non-Defaulting Party upon an Event of Default.
entities.evidence_quote: the Defaulting Party owed to the Non-Defaulting Party under this Agreement

entities.name: Event of Default
entities.legal_role: A triggering event that enables the Non-Defaulting Party to exercise set-off rights and other remedies.
entities.evidence_quote: upon the occurrence of an Event of Default, the Non-Defaulting Party shall have the right ... to set-off

entities.name: Set-Off Right
entities.legal_role: The contractual right allowing the Non-Defaulting Party to offset obligations owed by the Defaulting Party against obligations owed to the Defaulting Party.
entities.evidence_quote: the Non-Defaulting Party shall have the right ... to set-off any obligation of the Defaulting Party owed to the Non-Defaulting Party ... against any obligations of the Non-Defaulting Party owing to the Defaulting Party

entities.name: Commercially Reasonable Manner
entities.legal_role: The standard by which the Non-Defaulting Party may estimate unascertained obligations for set-off purposes.
entities.evidence_quote: If any such obligation is unascertained, the Non-Defaulting Party may in a Commercially Reasonable Manner estimate that obligation and set-off in respect of the estimate

entities.name: Performance Assurance
entities.legal_role: Security provided by the Seller to ensure performance of obligations under the Agreement, which must remain in full force and effect during the Security Period.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller shall ensure that any Performance Assurance, if required hereunder, remains in full force and effect and outstanding for the duration required by this Agreement

entities.name: Security Period
entities.legal_role: The period extending sixty (60) days after the later of the end of the Term or Seller’s full satisfaction of obligations, during which Performance Assurance must remain outstanding for the benefit of Buyer.
entities.evidence_quote: All applicable Performance Assurance ... shall remain in full force and effect and outstanding for the benefit of Buyer until sixty (60) days following the later of (a) the end of the Term or (b) the date on which Seller has fully satisfied all obligations to Buyer

entities.name: Buyer
entities.legal_role: The party entitled to benefit from the Performance Assurance and to exercise remedies if Seller fails to maintain Performance Assurance.
entities.evidence_quote: All applicable Performance Assurance ... shall remain in full force and effect and outstanding for the benefit of Buyer

entities.name: Seller
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to provide and maintain Performance Assurance and responsible for costs and replacement if Performance Assurance fails to meet requirements.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller shall ensure that any Performance Assurance ... remains in full force and effect ... Seller will be solely responsible for any and all costs incurred with providing and maintaining any Performance Assurance

entities.name: Event of Default Notice Right
entities.legal_role: The right of Buyer to notify Seller of an Event of Default if Seller fails to maintain Performance Assurance.
entities.evidence_quote: Buyer ... shall be entitled to give Seller notice of an Event of Default

entities.name: Termination Rights and Remedies
entities.legal_role: The contractual rights and remedies Buyer may pursue upon Seller’s Event of Default related to Performance Assurance.
entities.evidence_quote: Buyer ... shall be entitled to ... pursue the termination rights and remedies provided for in this Agreement

relationships.relationship_type: Set-Off Right Exercised Upon Event of Default
relationships.legal_significance: This relationship defines the Non-Defaulting Party’s right to offset obligations owed by the Defaulting Party against obligations owed to the Defaulting Party upon an Event of Default, enabling mitigation of credit risk.
relationships.evidence_quote: upon the occurrence of an Event of Default, the Non-Defaulting Party shall have the right ... to set-off any obligation of the Defaulting Party owed to the Non-Defaulting Party ... against any obligations of the Non-Defaulting Party owing to the Defaulting Party

relationships.legal_significance: Allows the Non-Defaulting Party to estimate unascertained obligations in a commercially reasonable manner for set-off purposes, ensuring fairness and reasonableness in the absence of exact amounts.
relationships.evidence_quote: If any such obligation is unascertained, the Non-Defaulting Party may in a Commercially Reasonable Manner estimate that obligation and set-off in respect of the estimate

relationships.relationship_type: Obligation to Provide and Maintain Performance Assurance
relationships.legal_significance: Seller is contractually obligated to provide and maintain Performance Assurance to secure its obligations under the Agreement, ensuring Buyer’s protection.
relationships.evidence_quote: Seller shall ensure that any Performance Assurance ... remains in full force and effect ... Seller will be solely responsible for any and all costs incurred with providing and maintaining any Performance Assurance

relationships.legal_significance: Performance Assurance must remain outstanding during the Security Period, extending Buyer’s protection beyond the Term or until Seller’s obligations are fully satisfied.
relationships.evidence_quote: All applicable Performance Assurance ... shall remain in full force and effect and outstanding for the benefit of Buyer until sixty (60) days following the later of (a) the end of the Term or (b) the date on which Seller has fully satisfied all obligations to Buyer

relationships.relationship_type: Right to Notify Event of Default
relationships.legal_significance: Buyer’s right to notify Seller of an Event of Default upon Seller’s failure to maintain Performance Assurance triggers potential remedies and termination rights.
relationships.evidence_quote: Buyer ... shall be entitled to give Seller notice of an Event of Default

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer may pursue termination rights and remedies if Seller fails to maintain Performance Assurance, providing a contractual enforcement mechanism.
relationships.evidence_quote: Buyer ... shall be entitled to ... pursue the termination rights and remedies provided for in this Agreement

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer benefits from the Performance Assurance as security for Seller’s obligations, ensuring financial protection.
relationships.evidence_quote: All applicable Performance Assurance ... shall remain in full force and effect and outstanding for the benefit of Buyer

extractor_notes: The clause combines two related but distinct legal mechanisms: set-off rights triggered by an Event of Default and Performance Assurance requirements with associated remedies. The set-off right is broad, allowing offset of matured or contingent obligations regardless of currency or place of payment, and includes a mechanism for estimating unascertained obligations. The Performance Assurance provisions impose ongoing security obligations on Seller, with a defined Security Period and explicit remedies for Buyer including drawing on the assurance, withholding payments, and termination rights.

source_clause_title: 5.2. Seller Covenants.  Seller covenants and represents and warrants to Buyer as of the Effective

entities.name: Seller
entities.legal_role: The party responsible for providing the covenants, warranties, and obtaining necessary approvals and agreements for the Facility under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller has obtained an approved and valid certificate of public convenience and necessity for the Facility from the Commission; ... Seller has obtained or shall obtain if determined to be necessary by Buyer, an Interconnection Agreement for the Facility ... Seller has secured from the Transmission Provider(s) the firm transmission required to deliver the Product to the Delivery Point; and ... Seller has obtained all applicable certifications and/or approvals for the Facility from FERC.

entities.name: Buyer
entities.legal_role: The party relying on Seller's covenants and warranties and entitled to terminate the Agreement and receive indemnification upon Seller's breach or failure.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller agrees and acknowledges that Buyer has entered into this Agreement in reliance upon the covenants and warranties set forth above in this section ... then this Agreement will terminate upon Buyer providing Seller with thirty (30) day’s written notice ... Seller will indemnify and hold Buyer harmless for any breach or failure relating to any of the foregoing covenants and warranties

entities.entity_type: Certificate of Public Convenience and Necessity
entities.name: approved and valid certificate of public convenience and necessity for the Facility
entities.legal_role: A regulatory approval that Seller must obtain from the Commission to lawfully operate the Facility.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller has obtained an approved and valid certificate of public convenience and necessity for the Facility from the Commission

entities.name: Interconnection Agreement for the Facility
entities.legal_role: A contractual agreement that Seller must obtain or will obtain if Buyer determines necessary, to connect the Facility to the grid.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller has obtained or shall obtain if determined to be necessary by Buyer, an Interconnection Agreement for the Facility

entities.name: firm transmission required to deliver the Product to the Delivery Point
entities.legal_role: Transmission capacity secured by Seller from Transmission Provider(s) to ensure delivery of the Product to the Delivery Point.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller has secured from the Transmission Provider(s) the firm transmission required to deliver the Product to the Delivery Point

entities.name: applicable certifications and/or approvals for the Facility from FERC
entities.legal_role: Regulatory certifications and approvals Seller must obtain from the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission for the Facility.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller has obtained all applicable certifications and/or approvals for the Facility from FERC

entities.name: Termination of Agreement by Buyer upon breach
entities.legal_role: Buyer's right to terminate the Agreement upon Seller's breach or failure of covenants and warranties after 30 days' written notice and opportunity to cure.
entities.evidence_quote: then this Agreement will terminate upon Buyer providing Seller with thirty (30) day’s written notice, unless such breach or failure has been cured before the end of such thirty (30) day period

entities.name: Seller's indemnification of Buyer
entities.legal_role: Seller's obligation to indemnify and hold Buyer harmless for any breach or failure relating to the covenants and warranties.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller will indemnify and hold Buyer harmless for any breach or failure relating to any of the foregoing covenants and warranties

relationships.legal_significance: Seller must obtain this certificate to comply with regulatory requirements and validate the Facility's operation under the Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: Seller has obtained an approved and valid certificate of public convenience and necessity for the Facility from the Commission

relationships.legal_significance: Seller must obtain the Interconnection Agreement if Buyer determines it necessary, affecting the Facility's grid connection and performance.
relationships.evidence_quote: Seller has obtained or shall obtain if determined to be necessary by Buyer, an Interconnection Agreement for the Facility

relationships.legal_significance: Seller must secure firm transmission rights to ensure delivery of the Product to the Delivery Point, which is essential for performance under the Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: Seller has secured from the Transmission Provider(s) the firm transmission required to deliver the Product to the Delivery Point

relationships.relationship_type: Obligation to Obtain Federal Regulatory Approvals
relationships.legal_significance: Seller must obtain all applicable certifications and approvals from FERC to comply with federal regulatory requirements for the Facility.
relationships.evidence_quote: Seller has obtained all applicable certifications and/or approvals for the Facility from FERC

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer has the contractual right to terminate the Agreement upon Seller's breach or failure of covenants and warranties after providing 30 days' written notice and opportunity to cure.
relationships.evidence_quote: then this Agreement will terminate upon Buyer providing Seller with thirty (30) day’s written notice, unless such breach or failure has been cured before the end of such thirty (30) day period

relationships.legal_significance: Seller is contractually obligated to indemnify and hold Buyer harmless for any breach or failure of the covenants and warranties, protecting Buyer from losses.
relationships.evidence_quote: Seller will indemnify and hold Buyer harmless for any breach or failure relating to any of the foregoing covenants and warranties

relationships.relationship_type: Reliance on Seller's Covenants and Warranties
relationships.legal_significance: Buyer entered into the Agreement relying on Seller's covenants and warranties, establishing the basis for Buyer's rights upon breach.
relationships.evidence_quote: Seller agrees and acknowledges that Buyer has entered into this Agreement in reliance upon the covenants and warranties set forth above in this section

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly conditions termination rights on a 30-day cure period and includes an indemnification obligation by Seller to Buyer for breaches of covenants and warranties; the Interconnection Agreement obligation is conditional upon Buyer's determination.

source_clause_title: 15.4. Required Disclosures.  Notwithstanding the confidentiality requirements  set forth herein, a

entities.entity_type: Confidential Information Subject to Disclosure Restrictions
entities.name: Protected Information
entities.legal_role: Protected Information is the confidential data subject to disclosure limitations and exceptions under the agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: Party may disclose Protected Information to comply with PURPA, request of any Governmental Authority, applicable Requirements of Law, or any exchange, control area or System operator rule, in response to a court order, or in connection with any court or regulatory proceeding.

entities.name: Receiving Party
entities.legal_role: Receiving Party is the party who receives Protected Information and has obligations to provide notice and limit disclosure under certain conditions.
entities.evidence_quote: To the extent the disclosure of Protected Information is requested or compelled as set forth above, the Receiving Party agrees to give Disclosing Party reasonable notice...

entities.name: Disclosing Party
entities.legal_role: Disclosing Party is the party who originally disclosed the Protected Information and has the right to seek protective orders to maintain confidentiality.
entities.evidence_quote: Receiving Party agrees to give Disclosing Party reasonable notice... and the Receiving Party shall not oppose such request or relief.

entities.name: PURPA
entities.legal_role: PURPA is a statutory requirement that may compel disclosure of Protected Information despite confidentiality obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: Party may disclose Protected Information to comply with PURPA...

entities.entity_type: Governmental Authority with Disclosure Request Power
entities.name: Governmental Authority
entities.legal_role: Governmental Authority is an official entity whose request may compel disclosure of Protected Information.
entities.evidence_quote: Party may disclose Protected Information to comply with... request of any Governmental Authority...

entities.name: Requirements of Law
entities.legal_role: Requirements of Law are legal mandates that may require disclosure of Protected Information.
entities.evidence_quote: Party may disclose Protected Information to comply with... applicable Requirements of Law...

entities.name: exchange, control area or System operator rule
entities.legal_role: These rules may require disclosure of Protected Information despite confidentiality obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: Party may disclose Protected Information to comply with... any exchange, control area or System operator rule...

entities.name: court order
entities.legal_role: A court order is a legal mandate that may compel disclosure of Protected Information.
entities.evidence_quote: Party may disclose Protected Information... in response to a court order...

entities.name: court or regulatory proceeding
entities.legal_role: A court or regulatory proceeding may require disclosure of Protected Information.
entities.evidence_quote: Party may disclose Protected Information... or in connection with any court or regulatory proceeding.

entities.name: reasonable notice of any discovery request or order, subpoena, or other legal process
entities.legal_role: The Receiving Party must provide reasonable notice to the Disclosing Party of any legal process requiring disclosure, enabling protective actions.
entities.evidence_quote: Receiving Party agrees to give Disclosing Party reasonable notice of any discovery request or order, subpoena, or other legal process requiring disclosure of any Protected Information.

entities.name: protective order or similar relief
entities.legal_role: Disclosing Party has the right, at its sole cost and discretion, to seek a protective order to prevent or limit disclosure.
entities.evidence_quote: Such notice by the Receiving Party shall give Disclosing Party an opportunity, at Disclosing Party’s discretion and sole cost, to seek a protective order or similar relief...

entities.name: disclose only that portion of the Protected Information that is required or necessary
entities.legal_role: If no protective order is obtained within 30 days, the Receiving Party must limit disclosure to only the necessary portion of Protected Information.
entities.evidence_quote: If such protective order or other appropriate remedy is not sought and obtained within at least thirty (30) days... Receiving Party shall disclose only that portion of the Protected Information that is required or necessary...

entities.name: reasonable efforts to obtain assurances that confidential treatment will be accorded
entities.legal_role: Receiving Party must use reasonable efforts to ensure that any disclosed Protected Information is treated confidentially.
entities.evidence_quote: Receiving Party shall use reasonable efforts to obtain assurances that confidential treatment will be accorded to any Protected Information so disclosed.

relationships.legal_significance: Receiving Party must notify Disclosing Party of any legal process requiring disclosure, enabling Disclosing Party to protect confidentiality rights.
relationships.evidence_quote: Receiving Party agrees to give Disclosing Party reasonable notice of any discovery request or order, subpoena, or other legal process requiring disclosure of any Protected Information.

relationships.legal_significance: Disclosing Party has the legal right to seek protective orders or similar relief to prevent or limit disclosure of Protected Information.
relationships.evidence_quote: Such notice by the Receiving Party shall give Disclosing Party an opportunity, at Disclosing Party’s discretion and sole cost, to seek a protective order or similar relief...

relationships.legal_significance: Receiving Party must not oppose Disclosing Party’s request for protective order or similar relief, facilitating confidentiality protection.
relationships.evidence_quote: Receiving Party shall not oppose such request or relief.

relationships.legal_significance: If no protective order is obtained within 30 days, Receiving Party must limit disclosure to only necessary Protected Information.
relationships.evidence_quote: If such protective order or other appropriate remedy is not sought and obtained within at least thirty (30) days... Receiving Party shall disclose only that portion of the Protected Information that is required or necessary...

relationships.legal_significance: Receiving Party must use reasonable efforts to ensure confidential treatment of any disclosed Protected Information.
relationships.evidence_quote: Receiving Party shall use reasonable efforts to obtain assurances that confidential treatment will be accorded to any Protected Information so disclosed.

extractor_notes: This clause establishes the limited exceptions to confidentiality obligations for Protected Information, specifying the procedural steps (notice, opportunity to seek protective order, limitation of disclosure) that Receiving Party must follow when disclosure is compelled by law or legal process.

source_clause_title: 20.2. Seller’s Failure to Deliver. If Seller fails to deliver Product that complies with the requirements

entities.name: Seller
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to deliver the Product in accordance with the Agreement and whose failure to deliver triggers remedies.
entities.evidence_quote: If Seller fails to deliver Product that complies with the requirements set forth in this Agreement or fails to deliver all or part of the Contract Quantity

entities.name: Buyer
entities.legal_role: The party entitled to receive the Product and to exercise remedies upon Seller’s failure to deliver.
entities.evidence_quote: then Buyer shall elect in its sole discretion

entities.name: Product
entities.legal_role: The goods or services Seller is obligated to deliver under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: If Seller fails to deliver Product that complies with the requirements set forth in this Agreement

entities.name: Contract Quantity
entities.legal_role: The specified amount of Product Seller is required to deliver under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: or fails to deliver all or part of the Contract Quantity

entities.name: Permitted Excuse to Perform
entities.legal_role: A condition that excuses Seller’s failure to deliver and prevents Buyer’s remedies from triggering.
entities.evidence_quote: such failure is not excused by a Permitted Excuse to Perform

entities.name: Event of Default
entities.legal_role: A defined breach event that allows Buyer to terminate and liquidate the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: if such failure is an Event of Default as set forth herein

entities.name: termination payment
entities.legal_role: The payment Buyer calculates and receives upon termination as the Non-Defaulting Party.
entities.evidence_quote: Buyer shall calculate its termination payment in accordance with this Agreement as though it were the Non -Defaulting Party

entities.name: liquidated damages
entities.legal_role: A pre-agreed monetary remedy Seller must pay Buyer for failure to deliver Product within a specified timeframe.
entities.evidence_quote: to require Seller to pay Buyer within three (3) Business Days of invoice receipt, liquidated damages in the amount obtained by multiplying the number of units of Product ... multiplied by two (2) times the per unit Contract Price

entities.name: Contract Price
entities.legal_role: The per unit price of the Product used to calculate liquidated damages.
entities.evidence_quote: multiplied by two (2) times the per unit Contract Price

relationships.legal_significance: Seller is legally obligated to deliver the Product as specified in the Agreement, failure of which triggers remedies.
relationships.evidence_quote: If Seller fails to deliver Product that complies with the requirements set forth in this Agreement

relationships.legal_significance: Seller must deliver the full Contract Quantity; failure to deliver all or part constitutes a failure to deliver.
relationships.evidence_quote: or fails to deliver all or part of the Contract Quantity (each will be deemed as a failure to deliver for purposes of calculating damages)

relationships.legal_significance: Seller’s failure to deliver is excused if a Permitted Excuse to Perform applies, preventing Buyer’s remedies.
relationships.evidence_quote: such failure is not excused by a Permitted Excuse to Perform

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer has sole discretion to elect remedies upon Seller’s failure to deliver, including termination or liquidated damages.
relationships.evidence_quote: then Buyer shall elect in its sole discretion: (i) to terminate and liquidate this Agreement ... or, (ii) to require Seller to pay Buyer ... liquidated damages

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer may terminate the Agreement if Seller’s failure to deliver constitutes an Event of Default.
relationships.evidence_quote: if such failure is an Event of Default as set forth herein, and in which case Buyer shall calculate its termination payment

relationships.legal_significance: Upon termination, Buyer is entitled to a termination payment calculated as the Non-Defaulting Party.
relationships.evidence_quote: Buyer shall calculate its termination payment in accordance with this Agreement as though it were the Non -Defaulting Party

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer may require Seller to pay liquidated damages within three Business Days of invoice receipt as a remedy for failure to deliver.
relationships.evidence_quote: to require Seller to pay Buyer within three (3) Business Days of invoice receipt, liquidated damages

relationships.legal_significance: Liquidated damages are calculated based on the number of units of Product Seller failed to deliver.
relationships.evidence_quote: multiplying the number of units of Product (or component thereof) that Seller failed to deliver

relationships.legal_significance: Liquidated damages amount is determined by multiplying the undelivered units by two times the per unit Contract Price.
relationships.evidence_quote: multiplied by two (2) times the per unit Contract Price

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly provides Buyer with two alternative remedies upon Seller’s failure to deliver: termination with a termination payment or liquidated damages payable within three Business Days. The clause defines failure to deliver broadly, including partial non-delivery, and excludes failures excused by a Permitted Excuse or Buyer’s own failure to perform.

source_clause_title: 10.1. Billing Period.  Subj ect to Seller authorizing Transmission Provider to provide Buyer with

entities.name: Buyer
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to read data from the Billing Meter, prepare and send invoices to Seller, and pay invoiced amounts within specified deadlines.
entities.evidence_quote: Buyer shall read/obtain data from the Billing Meter at regular intervals... Buyer shall provide Seller with an invoice... Buyer shall pay Seller the invoiced amounts...

entities.name: Seller
entities.legal_role: The party entitled to receive invoices and payment from Buyer for Product delivered during each Billing Period and obligated to cooperate with Buyer in providing data for invoice preparation.
entities.evidence_quote: Buyer shall provide Seller with an invoice... subject to Seller cooperating with Buyer and providing Buyer with such information and/or data... Buyer shall pay Seller the invoiced amounts...

entities.name: Billing Meter
entities.legal_role: The device from which Buyer obtains data to determine the amount of Product delivered during each Billing Period for invoicing purposes.
entities.evidence_quote: Buyer shall read/obtain data from the Billing Meter at regular intervals...

entities.name: Billing Period
entities.legal_role: The defined time interval, between 27 and 33 consecutive days, for which Product delivery is measured and invoiced, except for initial and final periods which may be shorter.
entities.evidence_quote: which shall be not less than twenty-seven (27) consecutive days and not more than thirty-three (33) consecutive days (each, a “Billing Period”) except for the initial and final billing periods hereunder which may be shorter...

entities.name: Invoice
entities.legal_role: The document prepared by Buyer and provided to Seller detailing the amount of Product delivered and the amount owed for each Billing Period.
entities.evidence_quote: Buyer shall provide Seller with an invoice detailing the amount of Product delivered during the relevant Billing Period and the associated amount owed...

entities.name: Payment Deadline
entities.legal_role: The date thirty (30) days after the invoice date by which Buyer must pay Seller the invoiced amounts to avoid interest accrual.
entities.evidence_quote: Payment by Buyer shall be due thirty (30) days after the invoice date.

entities.name: Interest Rate
entities.legal_role: The rate at which interest accrues on unpaid amounts from the payment due date until payment is made.
entities.evidence_quote: If such amounts are not paid by the deadline, they shall accrue interest at the Interest Rate from the applicable due date until the date paid.

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer is legally required to read or obtain data from the Billing Meter to determine Product delivery for invoicing.
relationships.evidence_quote: Buyer shall read/obtain data from the Billing Meter at regular intervals...

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer must prepare and provide Seller with an invoice detailing Product delivered and amounts owed within 25 days after reading the Billing Meter data.
relationships.evidence_quote: Within twenty-five (25) days after reading/obtaining data from the Billing Meter, Buyer shall provide Seller with an invoice...

relationships.legal_significance: Seller must cooperate with Buyer and provide requested information or data to enable accurate invoice preparation.
relationships.evidence_quote: subject to Seller cooperating with Buyer and providing Buyer with such information and/or data that Buyer may request to accurately prepare the invoice.

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer is obligated to pay Seller the invoiced amounts for each Billing Period by the payment deadline.
relationships.evidence_quote: Buyer shall pay Seller the invoiced amounts for each Billing Period. Payment by Buyer shall be due thirty (30) days after the invoice date.

relationships.legal_significance: If Buyer fails to pay by the payment deadline, interest accrues at the Interest Rate from the due date until payment is made.
relationships.evidence_quote: If such amounts are not paid by the deadline, they shall accrue interest at the Interest Rate from the applicable due date until the date paid.

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly defines the Billing Period duration, invoicing and payment obligations, and interest accrual on late payments, with clear roles for Buyer and Seller and their respective duties. The clause also references Seller's cooperation in invoice preparation, which is a key operational and legal relationship.

source_clause_title: 19.2. Effectiveness of Default and Remedies.  Where an Event of Default is specified herein and is

entities.name: Event of Default
entities.legal_role: An Event of Default triggers the possibility of early termination and other remedies under the agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: Where an Event of Default is specified herein

entities.name: Early Termination Date
entities.legal_role: The date on which the agreement is deemed terminated early due to an Event of Default.
entities.evidence_quote: an Event of Default and Early Termination Date shall be deemed to have occurred immediately upon any such event

entities.name: Non-Defaulting Party
entities.legal_role: The party not in default who is entitled to exercise remedies including acceleration, setoff, and early termination.
entities.evidence_quote: All of the remedies and provisions set forth in this section shall be without prejudice to any other right of the Non - Defaulting Party

entities.name: Defaulting Party
entities.legal_role: The party in default who is liable to pay the Net Settlement Amount following early termination.
entities.evidence_quote: The Defaulting Party shall pay the Non -Defaulting Party the full amount of the Net Settlement Amount

entities.name: Net Settlement Amount
entities.legal_role: The single net amount calculated by the Non-Defaulting Party representing gains, losses, and costs resulting from early termination.
entities.evidence_quote: a single net amount expressed in U .S . dollars (the “Net Settlement Amount”)

entities.name: Gains or Losses and Costs
entities.legal_role: Financial results calculated by the Non-Defaulting Party as a consequence of early termination.
entities.evidence_quote: calculate its Gains or Losses and Costs resulting from the termination as of the Early Termination Date

relationships.legal_significance: The occurrence of an Event of Default causes the Early Termination Date to be deemed to have occurred immediately, enabling termination without prior notice.
relationships.evidence_quote: an Event of Default and Early Termination Date shall be deemed to have occurred immediately upon any such event and no prior written notice shall be required

relationships.legal_significance: The Non-Defaulting Party is responsible for calculating the Net Settlement Amount in a commercially reasonable manner following early termination.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Non-Defaulting Party shall calculate its Gains or Losses and Costs resulting from the termination as of the Early Termination Date , in a Commercially Reasonable Manner

relationships.legal_significance: The Defaulting Party is obligated to pay the full Net Settlement Amount to the Non-Defaulting Party within five business days of notice.
relationships.evidence_quote: The Defaulting Party shall pay the Non -Defaulting Party the full amount of the Net Settlement Amount within five (5) Business Day s of delivery to the Defaulting Party of the notice

relationships.legal_significance: The Non-Defaulting Party calculates the Gains, Losses and Costs resulting from termination to determine the Net Settlement Amount.
relationships.evidence_quote: the Non-Defaulting Party shall calculate its Gains or Losses and Costs resulting from the termination

relationships.legal_significance: The Non-Defaulting Party retains all remedies including acceleration, recoupment, setoff, liquidation, and early termination rights under this clause.
relationships.evidence_quote: All of the remedies and provisions set forth in this section shall be without prejudice to any other right of the Non - Defaulting Party to accelerate amounts owed, net, recoup, setoff, liquidate, and early terminate this Agreement

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly defines the immediate effect of an Event of Default on early termination without prior notice, the calculation and payment of a Net Settlement Amount, and preserves the Non-Defaulting Party's broad remedies. The relationships focus on the flow of rights and obligations triggered by default and termination.

source_clause_title: 20.3. Buyer’s Failure to Accept Delivery.  If Buyer fails to receive all or part of the Contract Quantity

entities.name: Seller
entities.legal_role: Seller is the party entitled to elect remedies and receive payments when Buyer fails to accept delivery, acting as the non-defaulting party in such events.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller shall elect in its sole discretion either to: (i) terminate and liquidate this Agreement if such failure is an Event of Default as set forth herein... or, (ii) require Buyer to pay Seller within three (3) Business Days of invoice receipt, liquidated damages...

entities.name: Buyer
entities.legal_role: Buyer is the party obligated to receive the Contract Quantity and liable for damages or termination consequences upon failure to accept delivery.
entities.evidence_quote: If Buyer fails to receive all or part of the Contract Quantity that Seller attempted to deliver to Buyer in accordance with this Agreement...

entities.name: Contract Quantity
entities.legal_role: The specific amount of Product that Seller is obligated to deliver and Buyer is obligated to accept under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: If Buyer fails to receive all or part of the Contract Quantity that Seller attempted to deliver to Buyer in accordance with this Agreement...

entities.name: Permitted Excuse to Perform
entities.legal_role: A condition that excuses Buyer’s failure to accept delivery, preventing Seller’s remedies from triggering.
entities.evidence_quote: such failure by Buyer is not excused by a Permitted Excuse to Perform or Seller’s failure to perform...

entities.name: Event of Default
entities.legal_role: A defined breach condition that, if triggered by Buyer’s failure to accept delivery, allows Seller to terminate and liquidate the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: terminate and liquidate this Agreement if such failure is an Event of Default as set forth herein...

entities.name: Termination Payment
entities.legal_role: The payment Seller calculates and receives upon termination of the Agreement due to Buyer’s Event of Default.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller shall calculate its termination payment in accordance with this Agreement as though it were the Non-Defaulting Party...

entities.name: Liquidated Damages
entities.legal_role: A pre-agreed monetary remedy Seller may require Buyer to pay for failure to accept delivery, calculated as twice the per unit Contract Price times the units not received.
entities.evidence_quote: require Buyer to pay Seller within three (3) Business Days of invoice receipt, liquidated damages in the amount obtained by multiplying the number of units of Product... multiplied by two (2) times the per unit Contract Price...

entities.name: Contract Price
entities.legal_role: The per unit price of the Product used to calculate liquidated damages payable by Buyer upon failure to accept delivery.
entities.evidence_quote: liquidated damages in the amount obtained by multiplying... by two (2) times the per unit Contract Price...

entities.name: Invoice Receipt
entities.legal_role: The triggering event that starts the three (3) Business Days payment period for Buyer to pay liquidated damages to Seller.
entities.evidence_quote: require Buyer to pay Seller within three (3) Business Days of invoice receipt...

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer is legally obligated to receive all or part of the Contract Quantity delivered by Seller under the Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: If Buyer fails to receive all or part of the Contract Quantity that Seller attempted to deliver to Buyer in accordance with this Agreement...

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer's failure to accept delivery is excused if a Permitted Excuse to Perform applies, preventing Seller’s remedies.
relationships.evidence_quote: such failure by Buyer is not excused by a Permitted Excuse to Perform or Seller’s failure to perform...

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer's failure to accept delivery may constitute an Event of Default, triggering Seller’s right to terminate and liquidate the Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: terminate and liquidate this Agreement if such failure is an Event of Default as set forth herein...

relationships.legal_significance: Seller is entitled to calculate and receive a termination payment upon termination due to Buyer’s Event of Default.
relationships.evidence_quote: Seller shall calculate its termination payment in accordance with this Agreement as though it were the Non-Defaulting Party...

relationships.legal_significance: Seller has the right to require Buyer to pay liquidated damages within three Business Days of invoice receipt for failure to accept delivery.
relationships.evidence_quote: require Buyer to pay Seller within three (3) Business Days of invoice receipt, liquidated damages...

relationships.legal_significance: Liquidated damages are calculated based on two times the per unit Contract Price multiplied by the units not received.
relationships.evidence_quote: liquidated damages in the amount obtained by multiplying... by two (2) times the per unit Contract Price...

relationships.legal_significance: The obligation for Buyer to pay liquidated damages arises within three Business Days after Seller issues an invoice.
relationships.evidence_quote: require Buyer to pay Seller within three (3) Business Days of invoice receipt...

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly provides Seller with two alternative remedies upon Buyer's failure to accept delivery: termination with a termination payment or liquidated damages payment, with clear timing and calculation methods. The clause also conditions these remedies on the failure not being excused by a Permitted Excuse or Seller’s own failure to perform.

source_clause_title: 21.1. Reasonableness.  THE EXPRESS REMEDIES AND MEASURES OF DAMAGES, INCLUDING

entities.name: Liquidated Damages
entities.legal_role: This provision defines agreed damages as a reasonable approximation of anticipated harm, serving as a predetermined remedy for breach where actual damages are difficult to estimate.
entities.evidence_quote: TO THE EXTENT ANY PROVISION OF THIS AGREEMENT PROVIDES FOR , OR IS DEEMED TO CONSTITUTE  OR INCLUDE, LIQUIDATED DAMAGES, THE PARTIES STIPULATE AND AGREE THAT THE ACTUAL DAMAGES ARE DIFFICULT OR IMPOSSIBLE TO ESTIMATE OR DETERMINE, THE LIQUIDATED AMOUNTS ARE A REASONABLE APPROXIMATION OF AND METHODOLOGY  TO DETERMINE THE ANTICIPATED HARM OR LOSS TO THE PARTY

entities.entity_type: Express Remedy or Measure of Damages
entities.name: Express Remedy or Measure of Damages
entities.legal_role: This entity represents the specific remedies or damages expressly provided in the agreement for breach of provisions, which are intended to be the sole and exclusive remedies.
entities.evidence_quote: SUCH EXPRESS REMEDY OR MEASURE OF DAMAGES SHALL BE THE SOLE AND EXCLUSIVE REMEDY

entities.name: Obligor’s Liability Limitation
entities.legal_role: This limits the obligor’s liability to the amounts set forth in the express remedy provisions, excluding other remedies or damages at law or equity.
entities.evidence_quote: THE OBLIGOR’S LIABILITY SHALL BE LIMITED AS SET FORTH IN SUCH PROVISIONS

entities.entity_type: Waiver of Other Remedies or Damages
entities.name: Waiver of Other Remedies or Damages
entities.legal_role: This waiver excludes all remedies or damages at law or in equity other than those expressly provided, ensuring exclusivity of the agreed remedies.
entities.evidence_quote: ALL OTHER REMEDIES OR DAMAGES AT LAW OR IN EQUITY ARE WAIVED

entities.name: Waiver of Right to Contest Liquidated Damages as Penalty
entities.legal_role: Each party waives the right to challenge liquidated damages provisions as an unenforceable penalty, ensuring enforceability of such provisions.
entities.evidence_quote: THE PARTIES FURTHER STIPULATE AND AGREE THAT ANY PROVISIONS FOR LIQUIDATED DAMAGES ARE NOT INTENDED AS, AND SHALL NOT BE DEEMED TO CONSTITUTE, A PENALTY, AND EACH PARTY HEREBY WAIVES THE RIGHT TO CONTEST SUCH PROVISIONS AS AN UNREASONABLE PENALTY OR AS UNENFORCEABLE FOR ANY REASON

entities.name: Limitation to Direct Actual Damages
entities.legal_role: Where no express remedy is provided, liability is limited to direct actual damages only, which are the sole and exclusive remedy.
entities.evidence_quote: IF NO REMEDY OR MEASURE OF DAMAGES IS EXPRESSLY HEREIN PROVIDED, (i) THE OBLIGOR’S LIABILITY SHALL BE LIMITED TO DIRECT ACTUAL DAMAGES ONLY, SUCH DIRECT ACTUAL DAMAGES SHALL BE THE SOLE AND EXCLUSIVE REMEDY

entities.entity_type: Exclusion of Consequential and Other Damages
entities.name: Exclusion of Consequential, Incidental, Punitive, Exemplary or Indirect Damages
entities.legal_role: This excludes liability for consequential, incidental, punitive, exemplary, indirect damages, lost profits, or business interruption damages, even if allowed by law.
entities.evidence_quote: NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR CONSEQUENTIAL, INCIDENTAL, PUNITIVE, EXEMPLARY OR INDIRECT DAMAGES, LOST PROFITS OR OTHER BUSINESS INTERRUPTION DAMAGES

entities.name: Damages and Payment Obligations under Sections 20 and 21
entities.legal_role: This stipulation confirms that the damages and payment terms are a reasonable methodology to approximate harm or loss and waives challenges to enforceability or penalty claims.
entities.evidence_quote: Each Party expressly agrees and stipulates that the terms, conditions, and payment obligations set forth in Sections 20 and 21 are a reasonable methodology to approximate or determine harm or loss... and each Party hereby waives the right to contest such damages and payments as unenforceable, as an unreasonable penalty, or otherwise for any reason

relationships.relationship_type: Waiver of Penalty Challenge Attached to Liquidated Damages
relationships.legal_significance: This relationship ensures that the liquidated damages provisions are enforceable and not subject to being invalidated as penalties.
relationships.evidence_quote: THE PARTIES FURTHER STIPULATE AND AGREE THAT ANY PROVISIONS FOR LIQUIDATED DAMAGES ARE NOT INTENDED AS, AND SHALL NOT BE DEEMED TO CONSTITUTE, A PENALTY, AND EACH PARTY HEREBY WAIVES THE RIGHT TO CONTEST SUCH PROVISIONS AS AN UNREASONABLE PENALTY OR AS UNENFORCEABLE FOR ANY REASON

relationships.legal_significance: The express remedy provisions define and cap the obligor’s liability, limiting exposure to the agreed damages.
relationships.evidence_quote: SUCH EXPRESS REMEDY OR MEASURE OF DAMAGES SHALL BE THE SOLE AND EXCLUSIVE REMEDY, THE OBLIGOR’S LIABILITY SHALL BE LIMITED AS SET FORTH IN SUCH PROVISIONS

relationships.legal_significance: This relationship legally excludes all other remedies or damages beyond the express remedy, ensuring exclusivity.
relationships.evidence_quote: SUCH EXPRESS REMEDY OR MEASURE OF DAMAGES SHALL BE THE SOLE AND EXCLUSIVE REMEDY... AND ALL OTHER REMEDIES OR DAMAGES AT LAW OR IN EQUITY ARE WAIVED

relationships.legal_significance: This clause establishes that if no express remedy is provided, liability defaults to direct actual damages only, limiting exposure.
relationships.evidence_quote: IF NO REMEDY OR MEASURE OF DAMAGES IS EXPRESSLY HEREIN PROVIDED, (i) THE OBLIGOR’S LIABILITY SHALL BE LIMITED TO DIRECT ACTUAL DAMAGES ONLY

relationships.legal_significance: This clause excludes liability for consequential and other specified damages, limiting the scope of recoverable damages.
relationships.evidence_quote: NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR CONSEQUENTIAL, INCIDENTAL, PUNITIVE, EXEMPLARY OR INDIRECT DAMAGES, LOST PROFITS OR OTHER BUSINESS INTERRUPTION DAMAGES

relationships.relationship_type: Damages Stipulation Confirms Direct Damages as Recoverable
relationships.legal_significance: This confirms that damages under Sections 20 and 21 are direct damages and not excluded from liability or recovery under limitations.
relationships.evidence_quote: damages and payments determined under Sections 20 and 21 are direct damages, will be deemed to be a direct loss, and will not be excluded from liability or recovery under the Limitations of Liabilities provisions

extractor_notes: The clause extensively defines the exclusivity and reasonableness of liquidated damages and express remedies, waiving other remedies and challenges to enforceability, and limits liability to direct damages while excluding consequential and punitive damages. The clause also explicitly waives the right to contest liquidated damages as penalties, which is a critical enforceability safeguard.

source_clause_title: 23.5. Delivery of Assurances & Voidable .  Any Assignment or Change of Control will not relieve

entities.name: Seller
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to perform under the Agreement and whose obligations continue despite Assignment unless waived by Buyer.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller of its obligations hereunder, unless Buyer agrees in writing in advance to waive the Seller’s continuing obligations under this Agreement.

entities.name: Buyer
entities.legal_role: The party entitled to receive performance under the Agreement and whose written consent is required to waive Seller’s continuing obligations or to approve Assignments.
entities.evidence_quote: unless Buyer agrees in writing in advance to waive the Seller’s continuing obligations under this Agreement.

entities.name: Permitted Assignment
entities.legal_role: An Assignment allowed under the Agreement, requiring the assignee to assume Seller’s obligations and deliver assurances to Buyer.
entities.evidence_quote: In case of a permitted Assignment, such requesting party or parties shall agree in writing to assume all obligations of Seller and to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement and shall deliver to Buyer such tax, credit, performance, and enforceability assurances as Buyer may request

entities.name: Seller’s proposed assignee
entities.legal_role: The party proposed to receive an Assignment who must assume Seller’s obligations and obtain required approvals before Buyer’s consent.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller’s proposed assignee having first obtained all approvals that may be required by any Requirements of Law and from all applicable Governmental Authorities.

entities.name: Requirements of Law
entities.legal_role: Statutory or regulatory approvals required for Assignment or Change of Control to be valid.
entities.evidence_quote: all approvals that may be required by any Requirements of Law and from all applicable Governmental Authorities.

entities.name: Governmental Authorities
entities.legal_role: Regulatory bodies whose approvals are necessary for valid Assignment or Change of Control.
entities.evidence_quote: all approvals that may be required by any Requirements of Law and from all applicable Governmental Authorities.

entities.name: Change of Control
entities.legal_role: A transfer or sale event affecting ownership or control of the Facility or Agreement, subject to the same requirements as Assignment.
entities.evidence_quote: Any sale, transfer, Change of Control, and/or Assignment of any interest in the Facility or in the Agreement made without fully satisfying the requirements of this Agreement shall be null and void

entities.name: Event of Default
entities.legal_role: A breach event triggered by unauthorized Assignment or Change of Control, making Seller the Defaulting Party.
entities.evidence_quote: shall be null and void and will be an Event of Default hereunder with Seller as the Defaulting Party.

entities.name: Seller as Defaulting Party
entities.legal_role: The party responsible for the Event of Default resulting from unauthorized Assignment or Change of Control.
entities.evidence_quote: will be an Event of Default hereunder with Seller as the Defaulting Party.

relationships.legal_significance: The assignee in a permitted Assignment must assume all obligations of the Seller, ensuring continuity of contractual duties.
relationships.evidence_quote: such requesting party or parties shall agree in writing to assume all obligations of Seller and to be bound by the terms and conditions of this Agreement

relationships.legal_significance: The assignee must deliver tax, credit, performance, and enforceability assurances to Buyer, protecting Buyer’s interests.
relationships.evidence_quote: shall deliver to Buyer such tax, credit, performance, and enforceability assurances as Buyer may request

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer’s consent to Assignment is conditioned on the assignee obtaining all required legal and regulatory approvals, ensuring compliance.
relationships.evidence_quote: Buyer’s consent to any Assignment may be conditioned on and subject to Seller’s proposed assignee having first obtained all approvals that may be required by any Requirements of Law

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer’s consent to Assignment is conditioned on the assignee obtaining approvals from applicable Governmental Authorities, ensuring regulatory compliance.
relationships.evidence_quote: Buyer’s proposed assignee having first obtained all approvals ... from all applicable Governmental Authorities.

relationships.legal_significance: Any Change of Control without satisfying Agreement requirements is null and void and triggers an Event of Default, protecting contractual integrity.
relationships.evidence_quote: Any sale, transfer, Change of Control, and/or Assignment ... made without fully satisfying the requirements of this Agreement shall be null and void and will be an Event of Default

relationships.legal_significance: Seller is designated as the Defaulting Party upon occurrence of an Event of Default due to unauthorized Assignment or Change of Control, assigning liability.
relationships.evidence_quote: will be an Event of Default hereunder with Seller as the Defaulting Party.

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer’s written agreement is required to waive Seller’s continuing obligations under the Agreement, preserving Buyer’s protections.
relationships.evidence_quote: unless Buyer agrees in writing in advance to waive the Seller’s continuing obligations under this Agreement.

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly conditions Buyer’s consent to Assignment on assignee’s assumption of obligations and regulatory approvals, and establishes that unauthorized Assignment or Change of Control triggers an Event of Default with Seller as Defaulting Party, emphasizing strict control over transfer of interests.

source_clause_title: 6.3. Transmission Provider.  Seller agrees and acknowledges that the Interconnection Agreement

entities.name: Seller
entities.legal_role: Seller is a party obligated to perform under both this Agreement and the Interconnection Agreement, bearing sole responsibility for costs and compliance under the Interconnection Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller shall be solely and fully responsible for all costs and expenses for which Seller is responsible for under the Interconnection Agreement.

entities.name: Transmission Provider
entities.legal_role: Transmission Provider is a separate party to the Interconnection Agreement with Seller, but is not a party nor bound by this Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: Only the Interconnection Agreement will govern all obligations and liabilities set forth in the Interconnection Agreement... the terms of this Agreement are not (and will not) be binding upon the Transmission Provider.

entities.name: Buyer
entities.legal_role: Buyer is a party to this Agreement granted the right to obtain information from the Transmission Provider regarding Seller’s performance under the Interconnection Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller hereby grants Buyer the right and entitlement to obtain information from the Transmission Provider in regard to Seller’s performance under the Interconnection Agreement.

entities.name: Interconnection Agreement
entities.legal_role: A separate agreement exclusively governing all requirements, obligations, liabilities, and costs between Seller and Transmission Provider.
entities.evidence_quote: Only the Interconnection Agreement will govern all obligations and liabilities set forth in the Interconnection Agreement.

entities.name: Interconnection Instructions
entities.legal_role: Instructions under the Interconnection Agreement which Seller is obligated to comply with.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller shall comply with all Interconnection Instructions.

relationships.legal_significance: Seller and Transmission Provider are bound exclusively by the Interconnection Agreement which governs all their mutual obligations and liabilities.
relationships.evidence_quote: shall be a separate agreement (or agreements) between Seller and Transmission Provider, and will exclusively govern all requirements and obligations between Seller and Transmission Provider.

relationships.legal_significance: Seller is solely responsible for all costs and expenses under the Interconnection Agreement and must comply with its instructions.
relationships.evidence_quote: Seller shall be solely and fully responsible for all costs and expenses for which Seller is responsible for under the Interconnection Agreement. Seller shall comply with all Interconnection Instructions.

relationships.legal_significance: Transmission Provider is a party to the Interconnection Agreement and bound by its terms with Seller.
relationships.evidence_quote: Only the Interconnection Agreement will govern all obligations and liabilities set forth in the Interconnection Agreement.

relationships.legal_significance: Seller’s performance under this Agreement depends on Seller’s performance under the Interconnection Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: Seller agrees and acknowledges that Seller’s performance under this Agreement depends on Seller’s performance under the Interconnection Agreement.

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer is granted the right to obtain information from Transmission Provider regarding Seller’s performance under the Interconnection Agreement, enabling Buyer to monitor performance indirectly.
relationships.evidence_quote: Seller hereby grants Buyer the right and entitlement to obtain information from the Transmission Provider in regard to Seller’s performance under the Interconnection Agreement.

relationships.legal_significance: The Interconnection Agreement exclusively governs all obligations and liabilities between Seller and Transmission Provider, excluding this Agreement from affecting those rights and duties.
relationships.evidence_quote: Only the Interconnection Agreement will govern all obligations and liabilities set forth in the Interconnection Agreement... Nothing in the Interconnection Agreement, nor any other agreement between Seller on the one hand and Transmission Provider on the other hand... shall affect, alter, or modify the Parties’ rights, duties, obligation, and liabilities under this Agreement.

relationships.legal_significance: This Agreement does not create any rights or obligations binding upon the Transmission Provider.
relationships.evidence_quote: This Agreement shall not be construed to create any rights between Seller and the Transmission Provider, and the terms of this Agreement are not (and will not) be binding upon the Transmission Provider.

extractor_notes: The clause clearly distinguishes the Interconnection Agreement as a separate and exclusive contract governing Seller and Transmission Provider relations, while this Agreement governs Seller and Buyer relations. It explicitly denies any binding effect of this Agreement on the Transmission Provider and grants Buyer rights to information from the Transmission Provider, which is a notable cross-contractual information right.

source_clause_title: 8.1. Accounting Information.  Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (“GAAP”) and SEC rules

entities.name: Buyer
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to evaluate the economic relationship with Seller and to require financial information and access for accounting and audit purposes under GAAP.
entities.evidence_quote: Buyer may need access to Seller’s financial records and personnel in a timely manner.

entities.name: Seller
entities.legal_role: The party obligated to provide financial statements, schedules, and access to records and personnel to Buyer for audit and accounting purposes.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller shall grant Buyer access to records and personnel to enable Buyer’s independent auditor to conduct financial audits

entities.name: GAAP
entities.legal_role: The accounting standards that govern the preparation and consolidation of financial information required under the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: In the event that Buyer determines that consolidation or other incorporation of Seller’s financial information is necessary under GAAP

entities.name: complete financial statements, including notes
entities.legal_role: The quarterly financial reports Seller must provide to Buyer on a GAAP basis within 90 days after quarter end.
entities.evidence_quote: (a) complete financial statements, including notes, for such quarter on a GAAP basis

entities.name: financial schedules underlying the financial statements
entities.legal_role: Supporting documentation for the financial statements that Seller must provide to Buyer quarterly.
entities.evidence_quote: (b) financial schedules underlying the financial statements

entities.name: Buyer’s independent auditor
entities.legal_role: The auditor authorized by Buyer to conduct financial and internal control audits of Seller’s records in accordance with GAAP and Sarbanes-Oxley Act standards.
entities.evidence_quote: Buyer’s independent auditor to conduct financial audits (in accordance with GAAP standards) and internal control audits (in accordance with Section 404 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002)

entities.name: Section 404 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002
entities.legal_role: The regulatory standard governing internal control audits that Buyer’s independent auditor must follow.
entities.evidence_quote: internal control audits (in accordance with Section 404 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002)

entities.name: Information provided to Buyer pursuant to this section
entities.legal_role: Information subject to confidentiality obligations under the Agreement, limiting disclosure except as required by GAAP.
entities.evidence_quote: Any information provided to Buyer pursuant to this section shall be considered confidential in accordance with the terms of this Agreement

entities.name: other similar entities for which Buyer has power purchase agreements
entities.legal_role: Entities aggregated with Seller for disclosure purposes as required by GAAP.
entities.evidence_quote: shall only be disclosed, as required by GAAP, on an aggregate basis with other similar entities for which Buyer has power purchase agreements

relationships.relationship_type: Right to Access Financial Records and Personnel
relationships.legal_significance: Buyer’s right to timely access Seller’s financial records and personnel is essential for evaluating GAAP consolidation requirements and conducting audits.
relationships.evidence_quote: Buyer may need access to Seller’s financial records and personnel in a timely manner.

relationships.relationship_type: Obligation to Receive Quarterly Financial Statements
relationships.legal_significance: Buyer requires Seller to provide complete quarterly financial statements to comply with GAAP consolidation requirements.
relationships.evidence_quote: Buyer shall require the following for each calendar quarter during the term of this Agreement, within 90 days after quarter end: (a) complete financial statements

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer requires Seller to provide underlying financial schedules to support the quarterly financial statements.
relationships.evidence_quote: (b) financial schedules underlying the financial statements

relationships.legal_significance: Seller must grant Buyer’s independent auditor access to records and personnel to conduct required financial and internal control audits.
relationships.evidence_quote: Seller shall grant Buyer access to records and personnel to enable Buyer’s independent auditor to conduct financial audits

relationships.legal_significance: Buyer’s independent auditor must conduct internal control audits in compliance with Section 404 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act, ensuring regulatory adherence.
relationships.evidence_quote: internal control audits (in accordance with Section 404 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002)

relationships.legal_significance: Information provided under this section is protected by confidentiality terms of the Agreement, limiting disclosure to what GAAP requires.
relationships.evidence_quote: Any information provided to Buyer pursuant to this section shall be considered confidential in accordance with the terms of this Agreement

relationships.legal_significance: Confidential information may only be disclosed on an aggregate basis with other similar entities under Buyer’s power purchase agreements as required by GAAP.
relationships.evidence_quote: shall only be disclosed, as required by GAAP, on an aggregate basis with other similar entities for which Buyer has power purchase agreements

extractor_notes: The clause establishes Buyer’s rights to access Seller’s financial information and personnel for GAAP compliance and audit purposes, Seller’s obligations to provide quarterly financial statements and schedules, and confidentiality protections limiting disclosure except as required by GAAP on an aggregated basis. The clause explicitly references compliance with Section 404 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act for internal control audits.

source_clause_title: 13.1.1. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, Force Majeure will not include

entities.name: Force Majeure
entities.legal_role: Defines the scope and limitations of events that qualify as Force Majeure under the Agreement, which affects parties' obligations and rights during such events.
entities.evidence_quote: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, Force Majeure will not include the following: (a) any strike or labor dispute of the employees of either Party or any subcontractor that is not part of a regional or nationwide strike or labor dispute; (b) any difficulty in obtaining or maintaining sufficient, or appropriately skilled, personnel to perform the work in accordance with the requirements of this Agreement; (c) normal wear and tear or obsolescence of any equipment; (d) Buyer’s inability to economically use or resell the Product delivered and purchased hereunder; (e) Seller’s ability to sell the Product (or any component of the Product) at a more advantageous price; (f) loss by Seller of any contractual arrangement; (g) any Regulatory Event; (h) loss or failure of Seller’s supply of the Product or inability to generate the Product that is not caused by an independent Force Majeure event ; (i) the cost or availability or unavailability of fuel, solar energy, wind, or motive force, as applicable, to operate the Facility; (j) economic hardship, including, without limitation, lack of money or financing or Seller’s inability to economically generate the Product or operate the Facility; (k) any breakdown or malfunction of Facility equipment (including any serial equipment defect) that is not directly caused by an independent event of Force Majeure; (l) the imposition upon Seller of costs or taxes allocated to Seller hereunder or Seller’s failure to obtain or qualify for any tax incentive, preference, or credit; (m) delay or failure of Seller to obtain or perform any Permit; (n) any delay, alleged breach of contract, or failure under any other agreement or arrangement between Seller and another entity, including without limitation, an agent or sub-contractor of Seller (except as a direct result of an event of Force Majeure defined in 14.1(E)); (o) Seller’s failure to obtain, or perform under, the Interconnection Agreement, or its other contracts and obligations to Transmission Provider; or (p) increased cost of electricity, steel, materials, equipment, labor, or transportation.

entities.name: Party
entities.legal_role: A signatory to the Agreement who may be affected by or claim Force Majeure events impacting their contractual obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: any strike or labor dispute of the employees of either Party or any subcontractor

entities.name: Buyer
entities.legal_role: The party purchasing the Product under the Agreement, whose inability to economically use or resell the Product is excluded from Force Majeure.
entities.evidence_quote: Buyer’s inability to economically use or resell the Product delivered and purchased hereunder

entities.name: Seller
entities.legal_role: The party selling the Product under the Agreement, whose various failures or losses are excluded from Force Majeure except when caused by an independent Force Majeure event.
entities.evidence_quote: Seller’s ability to sell the Product (or any component of the Product) at a more advantageous price; ... loss by Seller of any contractual arrangement; ... loss or failure of Seller’s supply of the Product or inability to generate the Product that is not caused by an independent Force Majeure event

entities.name: Regulatory Event
entities.legal_role: An event related to regulation explicitly excluded from the definition of Force Majeure.
entities.evidence_quote: (g) any Regulatory Event

entities.name: Facility
entities.legal_role: The physical site or installation where the Product is generated or operated, whose equipment breakdowns not caused by Force Majeure are excluded from Force Majeure.
entities.evidence_quote: (k) any breakdown or malfunction of Facility equipment (including any serial equipment defect) that is not directly caused by an independent event of Force Majeure

entities.name: Permit
entities.legal_role: A governmental or regulatory authorization required for Seller to perform obligations, whose delay or failure to obtain is excluded from Force Majeure.
entities.evidence_quote: (m) delay or failure of Seller to obtain or perform any Permit

entities.name: Interconnection Agreement
entities.legal_role: A contract or obligation between Seller and Transmission Provider, whose failure to obtain or perform is excluded from Force Majeure.
entities.evidence_quote: (o) Seller’s failure to obtain, or perform under, the Interconnection Agreement, or its other contracts and obligations to Transmission Provider

entities.name: Notice of Force Majeure Event
entities.legal_role: The obligation of a Party rendered unable to perform due to Force Majeure to notify the other Party with details within specified timeframes, affecting rights and remedies.
entities.evidence_quote: If either Party is rendered unable by Force Majeure to carry out, in whole or in part, any material obligation hereunder, such Party shall provide notice and reasonably full details of the event to the other Party as soon as reasonably practicable after becoming aware of the occurrence of the event (but in no event later than three (3) Business Days of the initial occurrence of the event of Force Majeure). Such notice may be given orally but shall be confirmed in writing as soon as practicable thereafter (and in any event within ten (10) days of the initial occurrence of the event of Force Majeure)

relationships.relationship_type: Force Majeure Event Applicability to Party
relationships.legal_significance: Specifies that Force Majeure events affect the Parties' ability to perform obligations and trigger notice requirements.
relationships.evidence_quote: If either Party is rendered unable by Force Majeure to carry out, in whole or in part, any material obligation hereunder

relationships.relationship_type: Exclusion of Buyer Economic Inability from Force Majeure
relationships.legal_significance: Clarifies that Buyer's economic inability to use or resell Product is not a Force Majeure event, limiting Buyer's claims for relief.
relationships.evidence_quote: (d) Buyer’s inability to economically use or resell the Product delivered and purchased hereunder

relationships.relationship_type: Exclusion of Seller Economic and Contractual Failures from Force Majeure
relationships.legal_significance: Limits Seller's ability to claim Force Majeure for economic hardships or contractual losses unless caused by an independent Force Majeure event.
relationships.evidence_quote: (e) Seller’s ability to sell the Product (or any component of the Product) at a more advantageous price; (f) loss by Seller of any contractual arrangement; (h) loss or failure of Seller’s supply of the Product or inability to generate the Product that is not caused by an independent Force Majeure event

relationships.relationship_type: Exclusion of Regulatory Event from Force Majeure
relationships.legal_significance: Explicitly excludes Regulatory Events from qualifying as Force Majeure, affecting parties' rights and obligations.
relationships.evidence_quote: (g) any Regulatory Event

relationships.relationship_type: Exclusion of Facility Equipment Malfunction from Force Majeure
relationships.legal_significance: Excludes equipment breakdowns not caused by independent Force Majeure events from Force Majeure relief, limiting Seller's claims.
relationships.evidence_quote: (k) any breakdown or malfunction of Facility equipment (including any serial equipment defect) that is not directly caused by an independent event of Force Majeure

relationships.relationship_type: Exclusion of Permit Delay or Failure from Force Majeure
relationships.legal_significance: Excludes Seller's delay or failure to obtain or perform any Permit from Force Majeure, affecting Seller's obligations and remedies.
relationships.evidence_quote: (m) delay or failure of Seller to obtain or perform any Permit

relationships.relationship_type: Exclusion of Interconnection Agreement Failure from Force Majeure
relationships.legal_significance: Excludes Seller's failure to obtain or perform under the Interconnection Agreement from Force Majeure, limiting Seller's relief options.
relationships.evidence_quote: (o) Seller’s failure to obtain, or perform under, the Interconnection Agreement, or its other contracts and obligations to Transmission Provider

relationships.relationship_type: Party's Obligation to Provide Notice of Force Majeure Event
relationships.legal_significance: Requires a Party affected by Force Majeure to notify the other Party within specified timeframes, which is critical for triggering contractual rights and remedies.
relationships.evidence_quote: such Party shall provide notice and reasonably full details of the event to the other Party as soon as reasonably practicable after becoming aware of the occurrence of the event (but in no event later than three (3) Business Days of the initial occurrence of the event of Force Majeure)

extractor_notes: The clause explicitly excludes a broad list of events from the definition of Force Majeure, which narrows the scope of Force Majeure relief. It also imposes detailed notice requirements on Parties claiming Force Majeure, including timing and form of notice, which are critical for enforcement of rights and obligations.

source_clause_title: 13.5. Termination.  Unless otherwise agreed upon by the Parties in writing and in each Party’s sole

entities.name: this Agreement
entities.legal_role: The central contract governing the rights and obligations between the Parties, subject to termination and Force Majeure provisions.
entities.evidence_quote: this Agreement may be terminated without any further notice and further opportunity to cure any non-performance.

entities.name: Party
entities.legal_role: One of the contractual parties bound by the Agreement, entitled to rights and subject to obligations including termination and liability provisions.
entities.evidence_quote: neither Party will have any liability to the other Party or recourse against the other Party

entities.name: Party
entities.legal_role: The counterparty to the other Party, similarly bound by the Agreement and subject to the same rights and obligations.
entities.evidence_quote: neither Party will have any liability to the other Party or recourse against the other Party

entities.name: Force Majeure under Section 14
entities.legal_role: An extraordinary event or circumstance excusing performance and triggering termination rights without liability for the Parties.
entities.evidence_quote: Upon termination becoming effective pursuant to a Force Majeure under Section 14

entities.name: Event of Default not excused by the Force Majeure event
entities.legal_role: A breach or failure by a Party that is not excused by Force Majeure, permitting the other Party to liquidate and early terminate the Agreement.
entities.evidence_quote: including, without limitation, the right to liquidate and early terminate the Agreement for any Event of Default not excused by the Force Majeure event

entities.name: periods set forth above in Sections 14.4
entities.legal_role: Specified timeframes within which a Party may cure non-performance before termination can occur without further notice.
entities.evidence_quote: upon the expiration of the periods set forth above in Sections 14.4, this Agreement may be terminated without any further notice and further opportunity to cure any non-performance

entities.name: any right or remedy provided under this Agreement
entities.legal_role: The legal entitlements and actions available to a Party to enforce obligations or seek redress, even in the context of a Force Majeure event.
entities.evidence_quote: nothing herein shall relieve any Party from exercising any right or remedy provided under this Agreement

entities.name: strike, work stoppage or other labor dispute or disturbance
entities.legal_role: Events causing operational difficulties that do not obligate a Party to settle or make capital expenditures except at its sole discretion.
entities.evidence_quote: Nothing herein shall be construed so as to obligate any Party to settle any strike, work stoppage or other labor dispute or disturbance or to make significant capital expenditures

relationships.legal_significance: The Agreement may be terminated upon expiration of the cure periods without further notice, defining a condition precedent to termination.
relationships.evidence_quote: upon the expiration of the periods set forth above in Sections 14.4, this Agreement may be terminated without any further notice and further opportunity to cure any non-performance

relationships.legal_significance: A Force Majeure event under Section 14 triggers termination of the Agreement without liability between the Parties for post-termination obligations.
relationships.evidence_quote: Upon termination becoming effective pursuant to a Force Majeure under Section 14, neither Party will have any liability to the other Party or recourse against the other Party

relationships.legal_significance: Force Majeure does not excuse or suspend liabilities or obligations not covered by the event, preserving Parties’ rights and remedies under the Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: nothing herein shall relieve any Party from exercising any right or remedy provided under this Agreement with respect to any liability or obligation of the other Party that is not excused or suspended by the Force Majeure event

relationships.legal_significance: The existence of an Event of Default not excused by Force Majeure entitles a Party to liquidate and early terminate the Agreement.
relationships.evidence_quote: including, without limitation, the right to liquidate and early terminate the Agreement for any Event of Default not excused by the Force Majeure event
